attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who targets medium-mountain and uphill finishes rather than pure sprints. Demonstrates raw power but occasionally lacks tactical timing, particularly in break finishes where he leads out too early.
team_tactics: Often positioned as leadout support for primary sprinters (e.g., Girmay) but increasingly given freedom in breakaways. Team sets climbing tempo that eliminates pure sprinters while keeping him in contention for reduced bunch sprints.
terrain_preference: Excels on uphill finishes and medium-mountain stages. Survived Flemish climbs (Paterberg, Koppenberg) in elite group. Struggles in pure flat sprints against world-class finishers but competitive when gradient or attrition reduces field.
vulnerability: Sprint timing and tactical awareness remain inconsistent—led out from too far multiple times in breakaways. Dangerous in bunch sprints (caused Roglič crash). Lacks final acceleration against fresher or more experienced finishers despite superior sustained power.
key_patterns: Consistent breakaway podiums across Grand Tours, including multiple top-4 finishes at Vuelta 2022
Superior durability allows survival on team-imposed climbing pace that eliminates pure sprinters
Raw power evident but sprint timing immature: repeatedly leads out too early from breaks
Tactically cautious in jersey calculations, refuses chase duty when threatening GC math
Emerging time trial capability revealed alongside breakaway/sprint hybrid skillset
form_trajectory: positive: Evolution from pure breakaway opportunist to versatile threat combining TT ability, climbing durability, and sprint capacity across 2022-2023. Recent Dauphiné result shows continued progression.
